public_source_evidence: Public sources confirm GSE185061 is a PROTECT pediatric UC rectal methylation subset: 211 pediatric UC baseline rectal DNA samples, 73 follow-up rectal samples, and 85 RISK non-IBD controls; 369 post-QC samples. GEO sample titles expose labels such as PON0388UC_Base, POH0327UC_FU, and RS11028NotIBD.
crosswalk_blockers: DS038/GSE109142 uses generic public titles such as UC female1 rather than PROTECT subject IDs; DS039/GSE150961 uses Ind### identifiers; DS027/DS057 metabolomics use numeric or STL-prefixed numeric IDs. The existing PROTECT metabolomics crosswalk resolves only DS027-to-DS057 overlap, not methylation-to-transcriptomics or methylation-to-metabolomics overlap.
overlap_result: Same PROTECT family is confirmed, but public metadata do not provide a direct subject-ID crosswalk to current PROTECT transcriptomics or metabolomics rows.
recommended_overlap_notes: Public sources confirm GSE185061 is a PROTECT pediatric UC rectal methylation subset, with 211 UC baseline samples, 73 UC follow-up samples, and 85 RISK non-IBD controls. GEO exposes methylation sample labels such as P*/UC_Base, P*/UC_FU, and RS*/NotIBD, but public metadata do not provide a direct crosswalk to DS038/GSE109142 generic RNA-seq labels, DS039/GSE150961 Ind### RNA-seq identifiers, or DS027/DS057 metabolomics numeric/STL IDs. Keep cross-row subject overlap and component relationship as Unclear; do not sum PROTECT-family participants across methylation, transcriptomics, and metabolomics without manual subject-ID reconciliation or controlled/source metadata.
manual_review_notes: Most likely next step is a private/source-level PROTECT ID crosswalk. Public GEO matching proves same cohort family, not subject-level linkage.
